title: From Sunset to Star Rise by Christina Rossetti — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Go from me, summer friends, and tarry not:
I am no summer friend, but wintry cold,
A silly sheep benighted from the fold,
A sluggard with a thorn-choked garden plot.
Take counsel, sever from my lot your lot,
Dwell in your pleasant places, hoard your gold;
Lest you with me should shiver on the wold,
Athirst and hungering on a barren spot.
meaning: "From Sunset to Star Rise" by Christina Rossetti is a sad and nature poem consisting of 14 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Go from me, summer friends, and tarry not: I am no summer friend, but wintry cold,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Christina Rossetti's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
